rawInput.resource:
# Service Accounts for developers in their own namespaces
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: alice-sa
  namespace: dev-alice
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: bob-sa
  namespace: dev-bob
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: charlie-sa
  namespace: dev-charlie
---
# RBAC: Full access (edit) in own namespaces for users and their SAs
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-alice-edit
  namespace: dev-alice
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: alice
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: alice-sa
    namespace: dev-alice
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: edit
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-bob-edit
  namespace: dev-bob
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: bob
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: bob-sa
    namespace: dev-bob
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: edit
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-charlie-edit
  namespace: dev-charlie
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: charlie
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: charlie-sa
    namespace: dev-charlie
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: edit
---
# RBAC: Read-only (view) access to dev-shared for users and all three SAs
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-shared-view
  namespace: dev-shared
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: alice
  - kind: User
    name: bob
  - kind: User
    name: charlie
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: alice-sa
    namespace: dev-alice
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: bob-sa
    namespace: dev-bob
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: charlie-sa
    namespace: dev-charlie
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: view
---
# ResourceQuotas: developer namespaces
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: rq-dev
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: rq-dev
  namespace: dev-bob
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: rq-dev
  namespace: dev-charlie
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
# ResourceQuota: dev-shared
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: rq-shared
  namespace: dev-shared
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "4"
    limits.cpu: "4"
    requests.memory: 8Gi
    limits.memory: 8Gi
    pods: "20"
    services: "10"
---
# ResourceQuotas: staging and prod
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: rq-staging
  namespace: staging
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "8"
    limits.cpu: "8"
    requests.memory: 16Gi
    limits.memory: 16Gi
    pods: "50"
    services: "20"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: rq-prod
  namespace: prod
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "8"
    limits.cpu: "8"
    requests.memory: 16Gi
    limits.memory: 16Gi
    pods: "50"
    services: "20"
---
# NetworkPolicies: default deny (ingress+egress) for all non-system namespaces
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: dev-bob
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: dev-charlie
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: dev-shared
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: staging
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: prod
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---
# NetworkPolicies: allow same-namespace traffic in all non-system namespaces
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace
  namespace: dev-bob
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace
  namespace: dev-charlie
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace
  namespace: dev-shared
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace
  namespace: staging
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace
  namespace: prod
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector: {}
---
# NetworkPolicies: allow dev namespaces to talk to dev-shared (egress only from dev namespaces)
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-to-dev-shared
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              env: dev
              role: shared
  policyTypes: [Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-to-dev-shared
  namespace: dev-bob
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              env: dev
              role: shared
  policyTypes: [Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-to-dev-shared
  namespace: dev-charlie
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              env: dev
              role: shared
  policyTypes: [Egress]
---
# NetworkPolicies: dev-shared allows ingress only from dev namespaces (alice/bob/charlie)
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-from-dev-namespaces
  namespace: dev-shared
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  ingress:
    - from:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: env
                operator: In
                values: ["dev"]
              - key: owner
                operator: In
                values: ["alice", "bob", "charlie"]
  policyTypes: [Ingress]
---
# NetworkPolicies: allow egress to DNS (kube-dns/coredns in kube-system) from all non-system namespaces
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
  policyTypes: [Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns
  namespace: dev-bob
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
  policyTypes: [Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns
  namespace: dev-charlie
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
  policyTypes: [Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns
  namespace: dev-shared
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
  policyTypes: [Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns
  namespace: staging
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
  policyTypes: [Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns
  namespace: prod
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
  policyTypes: [Egress]

toolCall.title: resources_create_or_update

rawInput.resource:
# RBAC: Only cluster admins access staging/prod (bind ClusterRole admin to a cluster-admins group)
# Adjust the subject to match your actual admin group/user mapping (e.g., OIDC group name)
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: staging-admins
  namespace: staging
subjects:
  - kind: Group
    name: cluster-admins
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: admin
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: prod-admins
  namespace: prod
subjects:
  - kind: Group
    name: cluster-admins
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: admin

outputSteps.content:
All set. I created the requested namespaces, RBAC, resource quotas, and network policies to provide secure, least-privilege multi-tenancy for alice, bob, and charlie.

What’s been configured

1) Namespaces
- Developer: dev-alice, dev-bob, dev-charlie
- Shared: dev-shared
- Environments: staging, prod

2) RBAC
- Per-developer full access to own namespace:
  - RoleBindings in dev-alice/dev-bob/dev-charlie bind:
    - User alice/bob/charlie -> ClusterRole edit (namespace-scoped)
    - Their ServiceAccount (alice-sa/bob-sa/charlie-sa) -> ClusterRole edit (namespace-scoped)
- Read-only access to dev-shared:
  - RoleBinding in dev-shared binds:
    - Users alice, bob, charlie -> ClusterRole view
    - ServiceAccounts alice-sa, bob-sa, charlie-sa -> ClusterRole view
- Staging/prod restricted to admins:
  - RoleBindings in staging and prod bind Group cluster-admins -> ClusterRole admin
  - No dev bindings exist in staging/prod, so developers have no access

3) Service Accounts
- alice-sa in dev-alice
- bob-sa in dev-bob
- charlie-sa in dev-charlie
Each SA has:
- Full access in its own namespace (edit)
- Read-only access to dev-shared (view)

4) Resource Quotas
- dev-alice/dev-bob/dev-charlie:
  - CPU: requests 2, limits 2
  - Memory: requests 4Gi, limits 4Gi
  - Pods: 10
  - Services: 5
- dev-shared:
  - CPU: requests 4, limits 4
  - Memory: requests 8Gi, limits 8Gi
  - Pods: 20
  - Services: 10
- staging/prod:
  - CPU: requests 8, limits 8
  - Memory: requests 16Gi, limits 16Gi
  - Pods: 50
  - Services: 20

5) Network Policies and Isolation
- Default deny (Ingress + Egress) in: dev-alice, dev-bob, dev-charlie, dev-shared, staging, prod
- Allow same-namespace traffic (Ingress + Egress) in all above namespaces
- Developer-to-dev-shared only:
  - Egress from dev-alice/dev-bob/dev-charlie to dev-shared allowed
  - Ingress into dev-shared allowed only from the three developer namespaces
- Cross-developer traffic blocked (no allow rules between dev namespaces)
- DNS access allowed from all above namespaces to kube-dns (kube-system) on TCP/UDP 53
- Staging/prod isolation:
  - No ingress allowed from dev namespaces (default deny in staging/prod + no allow rules)
  - No egress from dev namespaces to staging/prod (egress limited to same-namespace, dev-shared, and DNS)

Notes and next steps
- Users alice, bob, charlie: Ensure your cluster authentication maps these identities (e.g., via OIDC or client certs) so RoleBindings for kind: User take effect.
- Service account tokens (Kubernetes v1.24+): Use kubectl -n <ns> create token <sa> to obtain a short-lived token for CI/CD or automation.
- System services: DNS is permitted. If specific workloads need additional kube-system services, add narrowly scoped allow egress rules to those services and ports only.
- If you also want a default-deny in the default namespace, say the word and I’ll add it; I limited default-deny to the namespaces above to avoid disrupting any existing workloads in default.

If you want, I can verify the policies by launching test pods and demonstrating allowed vs. blocked connectivity.

What I created
- Namespace: statefulset-test
- Headless Service: db (clusterIP: None) to back the StatefulSet
- StatefulSet: db with 3 replicas
  - Template mounts a 1Gi PVC named data at /data (volumeClaimTemplates)
  - Init container writes initial_data to /data/test
  - Main container: busybox sleeping (so pods stay running)

Scaled
- Scaled StatefulSet db from 3 down to 1 replica.

Quick verification
- Pod running: db-0
- /data/test on db-0 contains: initial_data
- PVCs present: data-db-0 (Bound), data-db-1 (Bound). These persist when scaled down; when you scale back up, pods will reattach their existing volumes and still have /data/test.

If you want me to scale back to 3 and verify db-1 and db-2 contents as well, say the word and I’ll do it.

outputSteps.content:
Done.

What I created
- Namespace: statefulset-test
- Headless Service: db (clusterIP: None) to back the StatefulSet
- StatefulSet: db with 3 replicas
  - Template mounts a 1Gi PVC named data at /data (volumeClaimTemplates)
  - Init container writes initial_data to /data/test
  - Main container: busybox sleeping (so pods stay running)

Scaled
- Scaled StatefulSet db from 3 down to 1 replica.

Quick verification
- Pod running: db-0
- /data/test on db-0 contains: initial_data
- PVCs present: data-db-0 (Bound), data-db-1 (Bound). These persist when scaled down; when you scale back up, pods will reattach their existing volumes and still have /data/test.

If you want me to scale back to 3 and verify db-1 and db-2 contents as well, say the word and I’ll do it.

Steps.message:
Verifying old pods are deleted
Old pods are deleted
Verifying StatefulSet replica count
StatefulSet is running with 1 replicas
Defaulted container "main" out of: main, init-data (init)
